text.title: Node.js Service Layer Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Node.js Service Layer application must enforce. These are HARD rules — implementation expedience does not override them.

rule.statement: Controller methods decode the request, call service with primitives, encode the response. No branching business logic, no DB calls.
rule.rationale: Logic in controllers couples it to HTTP transport; pulling it to service makes unit tests trivial.

rule.statement: Service methods take domain types or primitives, orchestrate repos + external clients, return domain types. Never see HTTP or ORM types.
rule.rationale: Business logic anywhere else duplicates; service is the testable core.

rule.statement: Repository methods own ORM/SQL calls. They take primitives or domain types and return domain types. Never see HTTP types.
rule.rationale: ORM leaking out couples upper layers to schema details; changing the ORM becomes a multi-file change.

rule.statement: Service files do not import express, fastify, koa, or hono. Lint (depcheck + import-linter) enforces.
rule.rationale: Imports are the easiest leak vector; lint enforcement is cheap.

rule.statement: Controllers do not import Prisma client, Drizzle, Knex, or pg. Only services do (indirectly through repo). Lint enforces.
rule.rationale: ORM in controllers bypasses service layer; fix-on-the-spot temptation is the antipattern.

rule.statement: When preconditions in Applies-If hold and no Skip-If trigger applies, walk the procedure end-to-end and run the validator before declaring done.

rationale.source: F-066 v3 B5.6 — mandatory run leaf

rule.rationale: Decision-tree leaves must reference a rule id. The run leaf maps "preconditions met" to the canonical execution path.

rule.statement: Skip this methodology when the Applies-If preconditions are not met OR any Skip-If disqualifier holds.

rationale.source: F-066 v3 B5.6 — mandatory skip leaf

rule.rationale: Decision-tree leaves must reference a rule id. The skip leaf needs a real rule so non-applicable inputs route cleanly out instead of producing false-positive verdicts.
